name: Delaware SNAP (Food Supplement Program)
description: Delaware's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to low-income individuals and families to purchase groceries. Administered by the Delaware Division of Social Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Delaware Medicaid
description: Delaware's Medicaid program (Diamond State Health Plan) providing comprehensive health coverage including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, dental, vision, and behavioral health services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL. Children: up to 213% FPL.

name: Delaware CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Provides low-cost health insurance to children in families that earn too much to qualify for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance. Covers medical, dental, and vision services.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income above Medicaid limits but below 213% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Family income between 142% and 213% of federal poverty level

name: Delaware Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to Delaware workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Delaware Department of Labor.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements

name: Delaware State Housing Authority (DSHA)
description: Delaware's housing finance agency providing affordable housing resources including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, housing development, and homelessness prevention services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income Delaware residents seeking housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 80% area median income

name: Delaware Volunteer Legal Services (DVLS)
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income Delawareans through pro bono attorneys in areas including family law, housing, consumer issues, bankruptcy, and estate planning.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Delaware residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Legal Aid Society of Delaware
description: Provides free legal assistance to low-income and vulnerable Delawareans in housing, family law, public benefits, disability, elder law, and education matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Delaware residents at or below 200% FPL with civil legal problems
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Delaware Division of Employment and Training
description: Provides workforce development services through Delaware JobLink including job search assistance, career counseling, skills training, and youth employment programs.
eligibilitySummary: All Delaware residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Delaware LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Helps low-income Delaware households pay heating and cooling bills. Provides seasonal fuel assistance and emergency energy crisis intervention.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Delaware households with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Delaware 211 Helpline
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Delawareans to local health and human services including food, housing, utilities, healthcare, childcare, and crisis intervention.
eligibilitySummary: All Delaware residents seeking community resource information
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Food Bank of Delaware
description: The state's largest hunger relief organization, distributing food to over 500 partner agencies including food pantries, meal programs, schools, and shelters across all three Delaware counties.
eligibilitySummary: Any Delaware resident experiencing food insecurity

name: Delaware Commission of Veterans Affairs
description: Assists Delaware veterans and their families with benefits claims, employment services, education benefits, healthcare access, and connections to state and federal veterans programs.
eligibilitySummary: Delaware veterans and their eligible dependents

name: Delaware Crisis Intervention Services (988 Lifeline)
description: Statewide crisis hotline providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress. Connects callers to local crisis services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Delaware experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: Delaware Purchase of Care (Child Care Assistance)
description: Provides childcare subsidies to low-income working families in Delaware to help pay for quality childcare while parents work, attend school, or participate in job training.
eligibilitySummary: Working families with children under 13 who meet income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Delaware TANF (A Better Chance)
description: Cash assistance program for low-income Delaware families with dependent children, providing monthly benefits along with employment services and supportive services toward self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children who meet work participation requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; very low thresholds

name: Delaware WIC
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and healthcare referrals for low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Delaware Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency upgrades to low-income Delaware homes including insulation, air sealing, heating system repairs, and energy audits to reduce utility costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: DART First State (Delaware Transit Corporation)
description: Delaware's statewide public transit system providing fixed-route bus service, paratransit, and specialized transportation. Reduced fares for seniors, persons with disabilities, and Medicare holders.
eligibilitySummary: All Delaware residents; reduced fares for seniors 65+, persons with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders
hours: Varies by route; generally 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM

name: Delaware Division of Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services for Delawareans with disabilities including career counseling, skills training, assistive technology, job placement, and on-the-job support.
eligibilitySummary: Delaware residents with physical, mental, or learning disabilities that impact employment

name: Delaware Division of Services for Aging and Adults with Physical Disabilities
description: Provides services for Delaware seniors and adults with physical disabilities including home-delivered meals, respite care, adult day programs, and long-term care options counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Delaware residents age 60+ and adults with physical disabilities

name: Wilmington HOPE Commission
description: City of Wilmington organization focused on violence reduction and community safety through intervention programs, youth development, job training, and reentry services.
eligibilitySummary: Wilmington residents, particularly at-risk youth and young adults

name: Ministry of Caring (Wilmington)
description: Provides emergency shelter, transitional housing, meals, and support services to individuals and families experiencing homelessness in the Wilmington area.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in the Wilmington area

name: Delaware Head Start
description: Comprehensive early childhood education for low-income Delaware families providing education, health screenings, nutrition, social services, and parent engagement for children birth to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children birth to age 5, pregnant women, and children in foster care
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 100% of federal poverty level; categorical eligibility for certain groups

name: Delaware Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for survivors of domestic violence in Delaware.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence in Delaware
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Disability Rights Delaware
description: The state's designated protection and advocacy agency providing free legal services to people with disabilities on issues including access to services, abuse and neglect, and civil rights.
eligibilitySummary: Delaware residents with disabilities facing rights violations or needing legal advocacy

name: Delaware GED and Adult Education
description: Provides free adult basic education, GED preparation, ESL classes, and workforce readiness programs for Delaware adults seeking to improve their education and skills.
eligibilitySummary: Delaware adults age 16 and older not enrolled in school

name: Latin American Community Center (Wilmington)
description: Provides social services, childcare, after-school programs, English classes, immigration assistance, and health education primarily serving the Latino community in Wilmington.
eligibilitySummary: Latino community members and other residents of Wilmington in need of services

name: Delaware Benefit Connection
description: Online portal to screen for and apply to multiple Delaware public assistance programs including SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, childcare assistance, and energy assistance in a single application.
eligibilitySummary: Any Delaware resident seeking to check eligibility for public assistance programs

name: Delaware Veterans Home
description: State-operated long-term care facility providing skilled nursing, rehabilitation, and assisted living services exclusively for Delaware veterans and their spouses in Milford.
eligibilitySummary: Delaware veterans needing long-term care and their spouses
incomeLimitNotes: VA eligibility requirements apply; cost sharing based on ability to pay
